title: Autograd, Seen
tagline: Why backprop is just local rules flowing through a graph
summary: A visual explainer for automatic differentiation that starts from five local gradient rules, walks a single computation graph forward and backward, then shows how modern frameworks hide the tiny ops inside larger modules and batch training.
highlights: See a single scalar graph reveal values on the forward pass and gradients on the backward pass, one stage at a time.
Keep the main graph clean while a separate ledger explains the exact upstream-times-local rule driving the current step.
Switch between atomic operations and framework-style modules to see what PyTorch or JAX are really hiding.
Explore a tiny three-example batch where the same parameters receive gradient contributions from multiple examples.
Export the current graph stage or the full timeline as JSON from inside the article.
